content.text: Standard dashboard templates give a GenAI team a common visual vocabulary from day one. Rather than every engineer authoring panels from scratch, a template catalog defines
the right panels, queries, and variable structures
for each operational context — then teams derive team-specific or feature-specific dashboards by overriding only what differs.

This topic covers the six core templates that cover the daily working surface of a production GenAI system: golden signals, cost accountability, quality health, incident triage, capacity planning, and model A/B comparison.

attrs.alt: Standard GenAI Dashboard Template Catalog — six templates by audience and purpose

content.text: Every template is structured around three invariants:
that determines which panels are primary vs secondary
calibrated to the operational cadence (10 s for incident triage; 5 m for cost)
— datasource, environment, model, and region — so every panel filter lines up
In Grafonnet each template lives in a separate
template is the first dashboard on-call engineers open during an incident. Its six panels map directly to the GenAI adaptation of the four golden signals.
— one from Alertmanager for firing alerts, and one from the deploy event datasource so latency spikes correlate visually with releases.
template is reviewed by engineering leads and finance weekly and at month-end. It answers:
what did we spend, on which features and models, and are we on track?
extrapolates current MTD spend using a linear projection
colours red above 80 % of the monthly budget threshold
tracks tokens-per-dollar over time to catch model pricing changes
ranks features by spend so teams can prioritise optimisation
Quality metrics differ from operational metrics: they come from
template visualises LLM-as-judge scores alongside user feedback signals.
eval score trend with deploy annotations
. A drop in quality score shortly after a model upgrade, prompt change, or retrieval index refresh is the primary signal of eval drift.

// Eval score trend with deploy annotations
local evalTrendPanel =
  g.panel.timeSeries.new('Eval Score Trend (LLM-as-Judge)')
  + g.panel.timeSeries.queryOptions.withTargets([
      promQuery.new(
        '$datasource',
        'avg(genai_eval_score{env="$env", model=~"$model"}) by (eval_dimension)'
      )
    ])
  + g.panel.timeSeries.standardOptions.withMin(0)
  + g.panel.timeSeries.standardOptions.withMax(1)
  + g.panel.timeSeries.standardOptions.thresholds.withSteps([
      { color: 'red',    value: null },
      { color: 'yellow', value: 0.7 },
      { color: 'green',  value: 0.85 },
    ])
  + g.panel.timeSeries.options.withTooltipMode('multi');
ensures that eval scores are statistically meaningful. If coverage drops below 5 % of total requests the quality scores become noise — this panel makes that visible.
via the Grafana link. It opens pre-filtered to the alerting time range and environment.
— faster than the default 30 s when actively debugging
— zoom in on the incident window, not historical trend
all error types as separate series
Upstream provider stat shows the most recent HTTP status code returned by the provider API
so the template opens at the alert's evaluation time.
template is reviewed weekly or when adding a new model or region. It answers:
when will we hit a rate limit or quota ceiling?
The most actionable panel is the
token rate vs rate limit percentage
. A threshold line at 80 % of the provider's TPM quota triggers a visual warning before actual throttling occurs.

# Token rate as % of rate limit quota
100 * sum(rate(genai_tokens_total{env="$env", provider="$provider"}[1m]))
  /
  scalar(genai_rate_limit_tokens_per_minute{env="$env", provider="$provider"})
reveals whether requests are approaching the model's context window. If the P95 context length is within 20 % of the model's maximum, prompt truncation or chunking strategy must be revisited.

# Predict token rate 30 days out (using 14-day trend)
predict_linear(
  sum(rate(genai_tokens_total{env="prod"}[14d]))[14d:1h],
  30 * 86400
)
When running a shadow experiment or canary model rollout the
template provides side-by-side signals. Both the control and candidate model variants are selected via the
Delta stats (Quality Score Δ, Error Rate Δ) are computed relative to the baseline model by subtracting one instant query from another:

# Quality score delta: candidate minus baseline
avg(genai_eval_score{model="gpt-4o-mini", env="$env"})
  -
avg(genai_eval_score{model="gpt-4o", env="$env"})
confirms that the A/B routing is behaving as configured — for example 10 % to the candidate. If this deviates, the experiment results are invalid.
Keep templates discoverable by enforcing a consistent folder and UID scheme:
source so that Grafana bookmarks, alert links, and Terraform references remain stable across re-deployments. Never let Grafana auto-generate a UID for a shared template.
defines all standard variables. Teams override values by passing keyword arguments:

// lib/variables.libsonnet
local g = import 'github.com/grafana/grafonnet/gen/grafonnet-latest/main.libsonnet';
{
  datasource:
    g.dashboard.variable.datasource.new('datasource', 'prometheus')
    + g.dashboard.variable.datasource.withRegex('/^prod-.*/')
    + g.dashboard.variable.datasource.generalOptions.withLabel('Data source'),

  env:
    g.dashboard.variable.query.new('env')
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.withDatasourceFromVariable(self.datasource)
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.queryTypes.withLabelValues('env', 'genai_requests_total{}')
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.generalOptions.withLabel('Environment'),

  model:
    g.dashboard.variable.query.new('model')
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.withDatasourceFromVariable(self.datasource)
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.queryTypes.withLabelValues('model', 'genai_requests_total{env="$env"}')
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.selectionOptions.withMulti(true)
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.selectionOptions.withIncludeAll(true),

  region:
    g.dashboard.variable.query.new('region')
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.withDatasourceFromVariable(self.datasource)
    + g.dashboard.variable.query.queryTypes.withLabelValues('region', 'genai_requests_total{env="$env"}'),
}
A feature team that runs multiple LLM features can
on top of the standard set without forking the template:
. This file exports panel constructors that embed the canonical PromQL queries. When a metric name is renamed across the org, updating this one file cascades to all templates simultaneously.
Before committing a template to the repository, validate two properties:
to valid Grafana JSON (structural check via
from the test environment (smoke test against a staging Prometheus)

# .github/workflows/dashboard-test.yml
name: Dashboard Template Tests
on: [pull_request]
jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Grafonnet
        run: |
          go install github.com/google/go-jsonnet/cmd/jsonnet@latest
          jb install
      - name: Render all templates
        run: |
          for f in templates/*.jsonnet; do
            jsonnet -J vendor "$f" > /dev/null && echo "OK: $f" || exit 1
          done
      - name: Lint rendered JSON
        run: |
          for f in templates/*.jsonnet; do
            jsonnet -J vendor "$f" | python3 -c "
import sys, json
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
assert 'uid' in d, 'Missing uid'
assert 'panels' in d, 'Missing panels'
assert len(d['panels']) > 0, 'Empty panels array'
print('LINT OK:', d.get('title'))
"
          done
When a new team deploys a GenAI feature, they need dashboards within the first sprint. The standard process:
Add the team's `feature` variable using the extension pattern shown above
Submit a PR — CI validates the Jsonnet renders and the deploy job pushes it to the staging Grafana org
After review, merge triggers the CD job to publish to production Grafana
The team sets alert notification channels in Alertmanager, not in the dashboard — keeping dashboards and alerting config separate
create dashboards by clicking in the Grafana UI. Any dashboard not in the repository will be overwritten by the next CD run. This rule enforces the GitOps contract and prevents dashboard sprawl.

Standard dashboard templates turn observability from an individual craft into a shared service. The six templates in this catalog — golden signals, cost, quality, triage, capacity, and A/B — cover the complete operational surface of a GenAI system.
Templates are Grafonnet Jsonnet files; teams derive overrides without forking
A shared panel library centralises PromQL so metric renames propagate everywhere
UIDs are pinned in source to keep alert links and bookmarks stable
All templates are validated and deployed via CI/CD; manual dashboard creation is prohibited
New teams onboard in one sprint by copying a template and adding one feature variable
